caps.proposition_text: The clinician observes right eye twitch.

evidence.span_text: Eyes: Reports right eye twitch. Denies right eye pain.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician denies right eye pain.

evidence.span_text: Eyes: Reports right eye twitch. Denies right eye pain.

caps.proposition_text: The patient is experiencing a tic.

evidence.span_text: - Medical Reasoning: The patient's father provided a video today as she did not have a twitch in office. It appears the patient is experiencing a tic.

caps.proposition_text: The patient exhibits excessive throat clearing.

evidence.span_text: - Patient Education and Counseling: Nature of the diagnosis was discussed today with the patient and her father. I advised eye tics are very common in children and as many as 1 in 5 children have a tick during their school years. We discussed tics can also include things like shoulder shrugging, facial grimacing, sniffling, excessive throat clearing, and uncontrolled vocalization. I explained that essentially they are brief, sudden and involuntary motor movements and that we do not have a full understanding of the cause. We also discussed that they typically occur around 5 to 10 years of age and mostly resolve on their own within a year. Dad was advised that treatment is not recommended as they do not appear to be bothering Karen and she continues to do well in school and activities. Additionally, we discussed avoiding drawing attention to the tic as it may cause Karen to feel self-conscious and increase the severity of the tic.
